title: Structural Vibration Fatigue Life Estimator
description: Real-time fatigue life estimator that samples structural vibration from an ADXL345 accelerometer and strain gauge, extracts peak amplitudes, accumulates Miner's rule damage using the Basquin S-N model, stores cumulative damage to FRAM, and transmits remaining life estimates via WiFi MQTT with alarm on D ≥ 0.8.
objective: Sample vibration at 100 Hz; accumulate Miner's rule damage every 60 s; store D to FRAM; transmit JSON telemetry via WiFi; alarm serial + MQTT when D ≥ 0.8; fault on sensor disconnect.
constraints: Arduino Nano + ESP32 UART bridge; ADXL345; INA125 strain amp; FRAM; WiFi MQTT; BOM < $55.
supporting_fact_titles: Miner's Rule Cumulative Fatigue Damage and S-N Curve Life Estimation
Fault Detection and Isolation in Embedded Propulsion Controllers
element_usage_notes: Z-axis acceleration sampled at 100 Hz; peak extracted over 1 s windows for damage input
Strain reading cross-validates acceleration; triggers fault if discrepancy > 500 µε at same load
Receives cumulative D and alarm state over UART; publishes JSON MQTT every 60 s
Cumulative damage D and cycle counts written to FRAM every 60 s; loaded at power-on

element_interactions.description: Damage computed from each peak amplitude is accumulated and written to FRAM every 60 s
Strain and acceleration readings cross-validated; discrepancy triggers fault
Arduino reads D from FRAM and sends to ESP32 via UART for MQTT transmission
ESP32 acknowledges successful MQTT publish; Arduino logs ack to FRAM event record
